ground_truth.title: Online Course Best Practices
ground_truth.main_content: There are numerous ways to teach an engaging, effective online course. By following basic best practices, instructors can optimize the online learning experience. There are numerous resources that can provide guidance about what makes an online course excellent, but two of the most frequently cited sources are the Quality Matters Rubric (Quality Matters, 2023) and the Rubric for Online Instruction (California State University, 2024). How to Get Started: Plan the components of your course. Check that all of your content helps your students achieve your learning objectives, and that your assessments tell you whether they achieved them. Create a course space in Moodle. Communicate early and often. Design content into small modules (e.g., by course week or unit topic). Include interactivity throughout the course so students can connect with you, the materials, and their peers. Be compassionate. Best Practices - Assessment: Ensure that learning objectives and assignments, tests, projects, and all other assessments are closely aligned. Include various opportunities for students to track their learning progress, including self-assessments such as quizzes and flashcards, along with peer review. Technology: Utilize current, readily obtainable course technology that promotes learner engagement and active learning. Include a variety of multimedia elements to accommodate different learning styles. Accessibility: Administer a brief survey mid-semester for students to provide feedback on the course. Make course materials easy to navigate and available in formats that meet the needs of diverse learners. Learner Interaction: Create learning activities that offer opportunities for student-student, student-instructor, and student-content interaction. Requirements for learner interaction are clearly stated. Resources cited: California State University, Chico (2024) Rubric for online instruction; Quality Matters (2023) Specific Review Standards from the QM Higher Education Rubric, Seventh Edition.
